This stage is cross-cutting support infrastructure: small crates and helpers that sit underneath startup, request processing, UI rendering, and shutdown-sensitive operations. Several pieces standardize async control flow and timing: async-utils lets any future be cancelled by a Tokio token, utils/readiness provides a one-way readiness latch with subscriber tokens, shell-escalation’s stopwatch turns a pauseable time budget into cancellation, and the TUI frame-rate limiter prevents redraw storms.

A second cluster prepares and renders images. utils/image ingests raw bytes or data URLs, validates formats, resizes and re-encodes safely, caches by content hash, and reports failures through its shared error type. core/image_preparation applies that logic to conversation history, replacing bad or oversized inline images with explanatory placeholders, while tools/image_detail and core/original_image_detail enforce model-specific “original detail” policy. For terminal pets, frames.rs extracts cached PNG frames from spritesheets, image_protocol.rs selects Kitty or Sixel output for the current terminal/tmux environment, and sixel.rs emits compact Sixel payloads.

The remaining utilities support execution ergonomics: sleep-inhibitor exposes platform-specific idle-sleep prevention backends, code-mode/runtime/value normalizes callback values across JSON/V8/image boundaries, replay_filter classifies replayed TUI events, cache provides lightweight LRU and SHA-1 helpers, sandbox-summary produces concise human-readable policy/config summaries, and v8-poc verifies the Bazel-wired V8 integration.
